title: Overhaul homepage visual coherence
description: Implement the approved Taste Skill v2 overhaul for projectcanopi.com: preserve palette, section order, URL structure, nav labels, form fields, logo, and legal copy while improving UX coherence, polish, typography, spacing, hero composition, CTA dock, and network presentation. Run em-dash, pre-flight, preservation, brand fidelity, tests, build, and visual checks.
close_reason: Implemented approved Taste Skill v2 homepage overhaul, dash cleanup, tests/build, and desktop/mobile visual audits.

title: Make homepage UI coherent and remove Canopi Web WIP state
description: Recompose the homepage so it reads as one coherent Canopi website experience rather than separate visual blocks. Canopi Web is now working, so remove the work-in-progress treatment and improve typography and section rhythm across the homepage while preserving the Canopi field-notebook direction and glossary terms.
design: Preserve the field-notebook visual direction and app palette, but reduce competing section treatments. Prefer a continuous page composition with shared spacing, typography, and border language over disconnected card-like blocks. Do not change the Canopi Web glossary; only remove stale WIP UI.
acceptance_criteria: Canopi Web button no longer shows Work in progress or equivalent WIP status. Homepage typography uses a coherent scale and rhythm across header, hero, download, network, and footer. Download and network sections feel visually connected to the hero rather than standalone cards. Existing domain language remains consistent with CONTEXT.md. Desktop and mobile layouts have no overflow or overlapping text. Tests and build pass.
close_reason: Removed the Canopi Web work-in-progress UI and stale localized status keys, updated the Web deployment follow-up note, and reworked homepage typography/download/network styling into one lighter field-notebook system. Verified JSON translations, npm test, npm run build, and desktop/mobile Playwright overflow checks.

title: Deploy Canopi Web to web.projectcanopi.com
description:
## Slice Summary
Create the dedicated Canopi Web production deployment at https://web.projectcanopi.com/ using a versioned Web Edition artifact from the main Canopi app repository.

## User Value
Users can open Canopi Web from the public website without coupling the app deploy to the marketing site build.

## Out of Scope
Do not move Canopi Web source out of the main Canopi repo. Do not duplicate app source in the website repo. Do not change Canopi Web feature scope.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Deploy Canopi Web on a dedicated Cloudflare Pages subdomain.

**Decision:**
Canopi Web production home is https://web.projectcanopi.com/. The deploy consumes a packaged Web Edition artifact produced by the Canopi app repo release pipeline. A small deployment repo or repo-owned CI wrapper may own Pages config, headers, redirects, artifact verification, smoke checks, and Cloudflare deployment, but not app source.

**Key interfaces:**
- App repo Web Edition build supports the subdomain root base path `/`.
- Release pipeline publishes a versioned Web Edition archive.
- Cloudflare Pages project has custom domain `web.projectcanopi.com`.
- Website CTA links to the subdomain and labels Web as work in progress until live.

**Out of scope:**
- Do not publish Web Edition under the marketing site `/app/` path as the primary path.
- Do not import Canopi app source into canopi-website.
- Do not add website-side catalog/search/storage behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A Cloudflare Pages project exists for `web.projectcanopi.com`.
- [ ] DNS/custom-domain setup resolves `web.projectcanopi.com` to the Pages project.
- [ ] Canopi Web builds with root base path `/` for the subdomain deployment.
- [ ] The deploy consumes and verifies a versioned Web Edition release artifact from the app repo.
- [ ] `https://web.projectcanopi.com/` renders Canopi Web.
- [ ] A representative browser route reload returns the Web app shell.
- [ ] Manifest, app assets, and catalog assets are served as real static files, not HTML fallbacks.
- [ ] Production smoke checks document and verify the deployment.

title: Decide production Canopi Web artifact source
description:
## Slice Summary
Choose the durable source for the packaged Canopi Web artifact used by production website deploys.

## User Value
Production deploys can include Canopi Web reliably instead of publishing a marketing-only site with a broken /app/ entry point.

## Out of Scope
Do not implement deployment automation in this decision bead. Do not change Canopi Web app behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** decision
**Summary:** Choose how production deploys obtain the packaged Canopi Web artifact.

**Current behavior:**
The public homepage is deployed, but the Canopi Web artifact is absent from production, so the Web CTA opens an Astro 404 page. Local preview can include the artifact when an archive path is provided, but the production artifact source is not settled in repository-owned configuration.

**Desired behavior:**
A human maintainer chooses one durable production artifact source and records the operational expectation clearly enough that automation work can proceed. Acceptable choices include a versioned release asset URL, a CI-downloaded artifact, a manually managed Cloudflare/CI environment variable pointing at an archive, or another explicit source with equivalent reliability.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canopi Web artifact contract: packaged archive with base path `/app/`, manifest, static app assets, and catalog files.
- Website build contract: marketing-only build must remain possible, while production Web deploy must include the artifact.
- Production deployment environment: whatever service provides the artifact path or URL to the website build.

**Out of scope:**
- Do not implement deployment automation in this bead.
- Do not change Canopi Web app behavior or catalog scope.
- Do not redesign the homepage.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A single production artifact source strategy is selected.
- [ ] The selected strategy states how the website build obtains a packaged Canopi Web archive.
- [ ] The strategy states who or what updates the artifact when a new Canopi Web release ships.
- [ ] The strategy preserves the boundary that Canopi Web source stays in the main Canopi app repository.
- [ ] Any required secrets, dashboard settings, or release assets are identified for downstream implementation.
close_reason: Decision: Canopi Web production should move to a dedicated Cloudflare Pages project at https://web.projectcanopi.com/. The app source remains in the main Canopi repo; production deploy consumes a versioned Web Edition artifact from the app release pipeline. The website should link to the subdomain and label the Web version as work in progress until the project is live.

title: Fix /app/ routing without swallowing Web assets
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix the Web Edition static routing contract so browser app routes serve the app shell while real Web Edition files remain directly served static assets.

## User Value
Canopi Web can be deployed without breaking app JavaScript, manifest JSON, or catalog files through an over-broad fallback route.

## Out of Scope
Do not import Canopi app source into the website. Do not add website-side catalog search, storage, Workers, Pages Functions, R2, KV, D1, service workers, or offline catalog behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Fix the Web Edition static routing contract without rewriting real Web Edition files to HTML.

**Current behavior:**
Local preview can serve `/app/`, but the current fallback strategy is too broad for at least some real app files. A request for the Web Edition manifest can be rewritten to the app shell instead of returning JSON. Production currently lacks the app tree entirely, but routing must be correct before production Web deploy is safe.

**Desired behavior:**
The website serves the Web Edition shell for `/app/` and client-side browser routes, while direct app assets, catalog files, and the Web Edition manifest are served as their real static files with appropriate content types.

**Key interfaces:**
- Public app entry point: `/app/`.
- Browser route fallback: app-owned client routes under `/app/`.
- Direct static assets: app JavaScript, CSS, logos, Web Edition manifest JSON, and Species Catalog files.
- Static hosting redirect/fallback behavior.

**Out of scope:**
- Do not add website-side catalog search, storage, Workers, Pages Functions, R2, KV, D1, service workers, or offline catalog behavior.
- Do not import Canopi app source into the website.
- Do not change the Canopi Web artifact contents except by consuming a new packaged artifact if one is intentionally supplied.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `/app/` returns the Canopi Web app shell in local preview.
- [ ] A representative client browser route under `/app/` returns the Canopi Web app shell in local preview.
- [ ] A representative `/app/assets/` JavaScript file returns JavaScript, not HTML.
- [ ] `/app/canopi-web-edition-manifest.json` returns JSON, not HTML.
- [ ] `/app/canopi-catalog/manifest.json` returns JSON, not HTML.
- [ ] At least one catalog data file returns static asset bytes directly.
- [ ] The marketing homepage and localized homepage routes still return normally.
- [ ] The fix is documented enough for a future deploy agent to understand why broad fallbacks are unsafe.
close_reason: Closed after product decision moved Canopi Web primary hosting to https://web.projectcanopi.com/. The subdomain deployment uses root-hosted app routing, so the website /app/ fallback is no longer required for the primary Web launch.

title: Publish Canopi Web in production deploy
description:
## Slice Summary
Make the production website deploy include the packaged Canopi Web artifact so https://projectcanopi.com/app/ opens Canopi Web instead of the Astro 404 page.

## User Value
Users can launch Canopi Web from the public website.

## Out of Scope
Do not redesign the homepage in this slice. Do not change Canopi Web feature scope.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Make production deploy include the packaged Canopi Web artifact.

**Current behavior:**
The public homepage reflects the latest marketing changes, but `https://projectcanopi.com/app/` and the direct Web Edition files return 404. This means production deployed the marketing site without the Web Edition artifact tree.

**Desired behavior:**
Production deploys publish the marketing site and the packaged Canopi Web artifact together. The public Web CTA opens Canopi Web, and direct app assets and catalog files are available from production.

**Key interfaces:**
- Production deployment command or dashboard build command.
- Web Edition artifact source chosen by the upstream decision bead.
- Website build command that installs the Web Edition artifact.
- Public smoke URLs for `/app/`, manifest JSON, app assets, and catalog files.

**Out of scope:**
- Do not redesign the homepage.
- Do not change Canopi Web feature scope.
- Do not bypass the packaged artifact contract by committing generated app output to the repository.
- Do not add website-side app or catalog behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Production deployment uses the selected packaged Canopi Web artifact source.
- [ ] Production `https://projectcanopi.com/app/` returns a successful response and renders the Canopi Web shell.
- [ ] Production Web Edition manifest returns JSON directly.
- [ ] Production app JavaScript/CSS assets return their real static content directly.
- [ ] Production catalog manifest and at least one catalog data file return real static asset content directly.
- [ ] The public homepage Web CTA opens the working Web Edition route.
- [ ] The deployment path fails loudly if production Web deploy is expected but the artifact is unavailable.

title: Add Web Edition deployment smoke checks
description:
## Slice Summary
Add repeatable checks that verify the Web Edition entry point and representative static assets are present after build or deploy.

## User Value
A deployment that omits Canopi Web or rewrites real assets to HTML is caught before users click a broken Web CTA.

## Out of Scope
Do not implement full Web Edition browser workflow tests here. Do not test Species Catalog behavior beyond direct static asset availability.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Add repeatable Web Edition deployment smoke checks.

**Current behavior:**
The Web CTA can point at a missing production app without an automated check catching the issue. Local preview was verified manually, but the production artifact absence was only caught by direct diagnosis.

**Desired behavior:**
There is a repeatable smoke-check path that verifies the Web Edition entry point and representative direct static files. The check should be usable locally against preview and against production after deploy.

**Key interfaces:**
- HTTP checks for the app entry point.
- HTTP checks for Web Edition manifest JSON.
- HTTP checks for a representative app asset.
- HTTP checks for a representative catalog asset.
- Existing package scripts or a small script entry point suitable for CI/manual use.

**Out of scope:**
- Do not implement full browser workflow testing for Canopi Web.
- Do not test Species Catalog query correctness here.
- Do not add deployment secrets or production artifact sourcing in this bead.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A repeatable command can check a supplied base URL for Web Edition availability.
- [ ] The command fails when `/app/` returns 404.
- [ ] The command fails when the Web Edition manifest is served as HTML instead of JSON.
- [ ] The command fails when a representative app asset is missing or served as HTML.
- [ ] The command fails when a representative catalog asset is missing or served as HTML.
- [ ] The command passes against a correct local preview with the Web Edition artifact installed.
- [ ] Documentation or command help states how to run the check against production after deploy.

title: Polish homepage preview and local Web test flow
description: Fix user-reported homepage issues after the edition redesign: uncropped demo video, remove the extra edition heading, make donation visible when Liberapay script fails locally, improve edition/download and Agroecology network UI using the Canopi app design system, and run a local preview that serves /app/.
design: Preserve the website field-notebook direction and the Web Edition artifact boundary. Use full-width video with object-fit contain and a stable dark frame. Use dense border-led app-system surfaces for edition and network sections, avoiding a broad redesign. Keep a local donation fallback link while retaining the official Liberapay widget snippet.
acceptance_criteria: Demo video displays without cropping. The 'Choose how to use Canopi' heading is removed. Donation is visible in the topbar even when the Liberapay widget script is blocked locally. Edition/download and Agroecology network sections use the Canopi app field-notebook design system more coherently. /app/ is testable locally via a preview/build-with-web flow, not npm run dev. Build/tests pass, Playwright verifies desktop and mobile layout plus /app/ availability.
close_reason: Fixed homepage polish issues, Web Edition preview route, and verified build/tests plus desktop/mobile Playwright checks.

title: Redesign homepage editions and agroecology network
description: Improve the Canopi website homepage with a focused edition comparison, demo video, donation button, Web Edition entry point, and Agroecology network section while preserving the existing field-notebook visual direction.
design: Use existing Astro/CSS/i18n patterns. Preserve current field-notebook aesthetic and avoid broad visual reset. Treat Canopi Desktop and Canopi Web as product editions; installer choices are secondary. Treat external organizations as an Agroecology network, not formal partners unless separately confirmed.
acceptance_criteria: Topbar removes the Features link and adds a Liberapay donation entry. Hero removes the introduction paragraph, replaces screenshots with demo-canopi-720.mp4, and presents Canopi Desktop plus Canopi Web CTAs. Desktop exposes more installer choices. Canopi Web links to /app/. Edition capability lists distinguish shared canvas features, Desktop-only features, and Web limited catalog. Updates/features release section is replaced by an Agroecology network grouped by country with listed organizations and logos. Web Edition /app/ integration remains static artifact based per existing integration doc. New visible copy is localized across all 11 locales. Build and tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented homepage edition redesign, demo video, donation widget, agroecology network, and Web Edition link verification.

title: Install local Codex skills in canopi-website
description: Install the Codex skills from /home/daylon/projects/skills into this project and verify the installed skill definitions are coherent, parseable, and reliable for future Codex restarts.
design: Inspect the local skills catalog before copying. Prefer project-local installation if this repo already has a Codex project layout; otherwise use the Codex skill install conventions. Preserve unrelated repo state and do not install deprecated or in-progress skills unless they are intentionally active.
acceptance_criteria: Project has the local skills installed in the appropriate Codex skill location. Each installed SKILL.md has valid frontmatter, required fields, clear trigger guidance, and referenced instruction files exist. Verification commands pass. Changes are committed and pushed.
close_reason: Installed 31 stable public skills from /home/daylon/projects/skills into ~/.codex/skills; patched flat-layout references and verified installed skill frontmatter, links, bundled files, and source catalog validation.

title: Update website for Canopi 0.9.2
description: Update the Canopi website for the GitHub v0.9.2 release at https://github.com/naejin/canopi/releases/tag/v0.9.2. Refresh release constants, download links, metadata, hero copy, feature copy, and release-note labels while preserving the existing site design.
design: Use the GitHub release notes as source of truth. Keep the old visual design unchanged. Update structured JSON with a parser to avoid invalid locale files.
acceptance_criteria: Website references Canopi 0.9.2 and release date 2026-07-08. Download URLs point to v0.9.2 assets for Linux, macOS, and Windows. All locale JSON files remain valid and contain updated release-specific copy. npm run build passes.
close_reason: Updated release metadata, localized release copy, and download links for Canopi 0.9.2; production build passes.

title: Polish mobile download options UX
description: Mobile users report that the More download options panel is not visible cleanly. Improve the mobile layout so the installer choices open in a polished, readable frame without being hidden or clipped by nearby content, while preserving desktop behavior.
design: Keep the existing Astro/CSS implementation and Canopi field-notebook palette. Prefer mobile-specific CSS adjustments over new dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: On mobile widths, opening More download options shows a fully visible, readable installer panel. The main Download Canopi Desktop and Open Canopi Web buttons keep stable shapes. Desktop behavior remains intact. Tests and build pass.
close_reason: Polished mobile installer panel visibility, compacted mobile rows, added mobile scroll assist, and fixed narrow header spacing

title: Refine Chrome-inspired landing layout
description: Implement a focused landing-page layout update inspired by the provided Chrome screenshot while preserving the Canopi field-notebook palette. Scope: centered donate button in header; right-aligned Discord, language, and theme controls; logo-above-headline hero; Desktop/Web CTAs with meaningful icons; improved installer options UX; coherent framed autoplay video; improved agroecology network logo visibility; footer preserved and refined.
design: Keep the current Astro component boundaries and existing translation keys where possible. Do not introduce a broad site redesign or new dependencies. Preserve Canopi ochre/parchment/ink palette and no-green UI-chrome rule.
acceptance_criteria: Header keeps Discord, language, and theme controls on the right; donate is centered in the top bar. Hero shows Canopi logo above the headline, improved typography, Desktop and Web CTAs with icons, and a clear more-options control under Desktop. Autoplay video renders below with a stable aspect ratio matching the asset. Agroecology network logos sit on optimized palette-aware backgrounds. Footer remains present. Local dev server runs for review before pushing.
close_reason: Implemented Chrome-inspired landing layout, expanded agroecology network, and cleaned unused code/assets

title: Sync project Taste Skill skills
description: Verify the project-local .codex/skills directory against https://github.com/Leonxlnx/taste-skill, add any missing upstream skills locally, and ignore project skill files in git.
close_reason: Synced project-local Taste Skill skills from upstream and ignored .codex/skills in git.

title: Install taste skill project-locally
description: Install https://github.com/Leonxlnx/taste-skill into this repository's project-local Codex skills directory only, without modifying the user-global ~/.codex/skills installation.
design: Use the Codex skill-installer helper with --dest pointing at /home/daylon/projects/canopi-website/.codex/skills so scope remains project-local.
acceptance_criteria: The taste skill is installed under the repo-local .codex/skills directory. No files are written to ~/.codex/skills. The installed skill contains a readable SKILL.md. Changes are committed and pushed.
close_reason: Installed Leonxlnx/taste-skill from skills/taste-skill into the repo-local .codex/skills/taste-skill directory only; verified SKILL.md frontmatter and confirmed no matching global ~/.codex/skills directory exists.

title: Update Liberapay donation account
description: Update the website header donation widget from the Naejin Liberapay account to the Canopi Liberapay account, covering the visible fallback link, widget script, and noscript fallback.
design: This is a funding endpoint update, not a product/domain vocabulary change; no CONTEXT.md update needed.
acceptance_criteria: Header donation widget loads https://liberapay.com/Canopi/widgets/button.js. Fallback and noscript donation links point to https://liberapay.com/Canopi/donate. No Naejin Liberapay links remain in source. Tests and build pass.
close_reason: Updated header Liberapay widget, fallback, and noscript donation links to the Canopi account; verified npm test, npm run build, and source search.

title: Simplify homepage download section
description: Simplify the homepage download section so the path to trying Canopi is immediate: two main actions for Canopi Desktop and Canopi Web, with platform-specific Desktop installers hidden behind a minimalist secondary disclosure. Preserve product terminology from CONTEXT.md.
design: Use the glossary terms Canopi Desktop and Canopi Web. Treat platform installers as secondary choices, not products. Keep the section visually minimal and modern using the existing app palette tokens; optimize for a smooth first action rather than comparison.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Download section presents exactly two primary visible user actions: Canopi Desktop and Canopi Web.
- [ ] Canopi Desktop remains the primary/default action with OS-detected installer behavior.
- [ ] Additional Desktop installers remain available through a minimalist secondary disclosure.
- [ ] Canopi Web remains visible as work in progress and links to https://web.projectcanopi.com/.
- [ ] Capability/feature grids are removed from the download section.
- [ ] Desktop and mobile layouts have no overflow or overlapping text.
- [ ] Tests and build pass.
close_reason: Simplified the homepage download section to two primary actions with secondary installer disclosure; verified npm test, npm run build, and desktop/mobile Playwright overflow checks.

title: Rework download and network visual design
description: Rework the Canopi Desktop/Web download section and the Agroecology network section as two separate, fully redesigned website sections. Use the Canopi app color palette but do not follow the rest of system.md structure. Preserve glossary terms and current links/data.
design: Apply the frontend-design guidance: structure must encode the actual content, not decorate it. Keep the full-bleed video as the hero signature; redesign the download section as a primary edition dock and the network section as a separate country atlas/index. Use only the app palette tokens already present in the site; ignore the rest of system.md layout prescriptions for this pass.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Download and network remain two separate sections.
- [ ] Download section presents Canopi Desktop as primary and Canopi Web as secondary/work-in-progress.
- [ ] Desktop installer choices remain secondary.
- [ ] Feature language remains consistent with CONTEXT.md.
- [ ] Agroecology network remains country-led and does not imply partnership.
- [ ] Design uses the app color palette and feels intentional rather than generic card/ledger UI.
- [ ] Desktop and mobile screenshots show no overflow or overlap.
- [ ] Tests and build pass.
close_reason: Reworked the download section as a primary Desktop edition dock with secondary Web strip, and reworked the Agroecology network as a separate country-led atlas. Verified tests, build, screenshots, and overflow checks.

title: Document Canopi Web subdomain deployment
description: Write a detailed markdown guide explaining how to make Canopi Web work at https://web.projectcanopi.com using the app repo Web Edition artifact, a dedicated Cloudflare Pages deployment, DNS/custom domain setup, redirects, artifact validation, CI, and smoke checks.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A markdown deployment guide exists in the website repo.
- [ ] The guide targets https://web.projectcanopi.com, not the old /app/ marketing-site embedding plan.
- [ ] The guide explains artifact production, static hosting shape, Cloudflare Pages project setup, DNS/custom domain setup, redirects, cache headers, CI secrets, and smoke checks.
- [ ] The guide records project boundaries and failure modes.
close_reason: Added docs/canopi-web-subdomain-deployment.md with the subdomain deployment runbook, artifact contract, Cloudflare setup, routing, caching, verification, and smoke checks.

title: Refine edition rail UI
description: Rebuild the Canopi Desktop / Canopi Web download area so it fits the existing field-notebook website design. Preserve the agreed product language: Desktop and Web are the two editions, platform installers are secondary, Web remains work in progress, and features use concise names such as Canvas tools.
design: Use the Canopi field-notebook system: warm parchment/ink/ochre palette, ruled borders instead of heavy cards, Inter/Lora typography, no green UI chrome, and compact tool-like controls. Apply the frontend-design guidance by spending distinctiveness on a single edition-rail structure and keeping supporting details disciplined.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Edition/download section is full-width and visually integrated with the surrounding hero and Agroecology network sections.
- [ ] Desktop remains the primary action with additional installer choices available.
- [ ] Web is labeled as work in progress and links to https://web.projectcanopi.com/.
- [ ] Feature names remain concise and consistent with CONTEXT.md.
- [ ] Responsive screenshots and overflow checks pass.
close_reason: Implemented full-width edition rail and verified build, tests, screenshots, and overflow checks.

title: Refine Agroecology network section
description:
## Slice Summary
Improve the Agroecology network section as a coherent country-ledger/index surface using the Canopi field-notebook design language.

## User Value
The network reads as part of Canopi's system, not a separate collection of logo cards, while preserving the non-partnership terminology.

## Out of Scope
Do not add new organizations or imply partnership, endorsement, customer status, or formal affiliation. Do not redesign unrelated homepage sections.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Refine the Agroecology network section as a country-ledger/index surface.

**Current behavior:**
The network section groups organizations by country with logos and names, but the visual treatment can still feel like a collection of logo cards rather than a single Canopi system surface.

**Desired behavior:**
The section should read as an Agroecology network index: country-led, ruled, calm, and aligned. Organization logos and names remain visible, but the layout should feel integrated with the Canopi field-notebook design language.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canonical term: Agroecology network.
- Country grouping for France and Brazil.
- Organization link list with logos and names.
- Field-notebook design system: parchment/ochre/ink palette, border-led structure, dense spacing, no green UI chrome.
- Frontend-design critique lens: supporting UI should be distinctive to Canopi, not generic logo-card marketing.

**Out of scope:**
- Do not add new organizations.
- Do not imply partnership, endorsement, customer relationship, or formal affiliation.
- Do not add descriptive copy under the section title.
- Do not redesign unrelated homepage sections.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The section title remains Agroecology network with no descriptor copy.
- [ ] Organizations remain grouped by country.
- [ ] Each organization has a visible logo, name, and external link.
- [ ] The visual treatment reads as one coherent network/index surface rather than separate marketing cards.
- [ ] The section preserves the non-partnership language from the project context.
- [ ] Desktop/mobile screenshots show no text overflow, overlap, or horizontal scrolling.
- [ ] Existing localization coverage is preserved for any changed visible copy.
close_reason: Implemented the Agroecology network as a country-led ledger: title remains descriptor-free, countries group the existing organizations, logos/names/links remain visible, and the surface reads as one ruled Canopi index. Verified npm test, npm run build, Playwright desktop/mobile screenshots, and no horizontal overflow.

title: Refine Canopi Desktop and Web edition section
description:
## Slice Summary
Improve the Canopi Desktop and Canopi Web section as a compact edition workbench with clearer CTA hierarchy and feature comparison.

## User Value
Users can quickly choose Desktop or Web and understand the capability difference without the section feeling like a loose collection of cards.

## Out of Scope
Do not change download URLs, release metadata, or Web Edition deployment mechanics. Do not broaden the homepage visual redesign beyond this section.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Refine the Canopi Desktop and Canopi Web section as a compact edition workbench.

**Current behavior:**
The section presents two edition panels with CTAs and feature chips, but it can read as generic cards instead of a precise Canopi product-choice surface.

**Desired behavior:**
The section should feel like part of the Canopi field-notebook tool system: compact, ruled, aligned, and easy to scan. Canopi Desktop and Canopi Web remain the canonical edition names. Desktop installer choices are secondary. Canopi Web links to `https://web.projectcanopi.com/` and is visibly marked as work in progress until the dedicated Pages project is live.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canopi Desktop CTA and installer choice disclosure.
- Canopi Web CTA to `https://web.projectcanopi.com/`.
- Web work-in-progress status label.
- Edition capability language: Canvas tools, full plant database, limited plant catalog, and Desktop-only capabilities.
- Field-notebook design system: parchment/ochre/ink palette, border-led structure, dense spacing, no green UI chrome.
- Frontend-design critique lens: one strong signature element already exists in the full-bleed video, so supporting UI should be restrained and subject-specific.

**Out of scope:**
- Do not change release download URLs or release metadata.
- Do not deploy Canopi Web in this website UI slice.
- Do not broaden into a full homepage redesign.
- Do not rename Canopi Desktop or Canopi Web.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The section clearly presents Canopi Desktop and Canopi Web as two editions.
- [ ] Desktop has one primary CTA with platform installer choices kept secondary.
- [ ] Web has one CTA pointing to `https://web.projectcanopi.com/`.
- [ ] Web is visibly labelled as work in progress.
- [ ] Shared and edition-specific capabilities are easier to compare than the current chip-only presentation.
- [ ] The section follows the Canopi field-notebook design language with restrained borders, tight spacing, and no green UI chrome.
- [ ] Desktop/mobile screenshots show no text overflow, overlap, or horizontal scrolling.
- [ ] Existing localization coverage is preserved for any changed visible copy.
close_reason: Implemented the edition ledger UI: Desktop keeps one primary detected installer CTA with secondary choices, Web points to https://web.projectcanopi.com/ and shows a work-in-progress label, and feature rows compare shared, Desktop-only, and Web capabilities. Verified npm test, npm run build, Playwright desktop/mobile screenshots, and no horizontal overflow.

title: Keep the existing website visual design
description: After reviewing the broad visual redesign pass from canopi-website-t83, the team decided to discard it because users prefer the previous Canopi website design. Future design work should preserve the old visual direction unless a narrower, user-backed change is requested.
close_reason: Accepted decision: preserve the previous visual design after review.

title: Upgrade website visual design
description: Apply the redesign-existing-projects skill to the Canopi website. Audit current Astro/CSS design patterns, then make focused visual upgrades without changing existing functionality or unrelated release/translation work.
design: Use the redesign-existing-projects scan/diagnose/fix workflow. Focus on typography presence, layout rhythm, button states, section depth, and avoiding generic card-heavy patterns while preserving the Canopi field-notebook brand.
acceptance_criteria: Astro build passes. Visual upgrades stay within existing Astro and CSS stack. Existing i18n and download behavior remains intact. Changes are limited to design polish and necessary accessibility or interaction improvements.
notes: Housekeeping: visual redesign was reverted after local review because users prefer the previous version. Keep the old site design for now; any future redesign should be filed as a new bead with a narrower proposal.
close_reason: Completed targeted redesign pass; production build and Playwright desktop/mobile checks passed.

value: Canopi website: broad visual redesign was reverted on 2026-07-07 because users prefer the previous design. Preserve the old visual direction unless a narrower user-backed change is requested.
